No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a repo maintainer enforcing secure configuration for your team's jj repository.
You need to isolate the repository's configuration so that it overrides the global configuration with specific security settings.

Specifically, you must:
1. Initialize a new jj repository in `/home/user/secure_repo`.
2. Configure the repository-level config (`.jj/repo/config.toml`) to enforce signing by setting `signing.behavior = "own"` and `signing.backend = "gpg"`.
3. Set the repository-level user name to "Security Admin" and email to "admin@security.local".
4. Create a new commit with an empty file named `audit.log` and verify that `jj log` shows the commit successfully.

Please complete these steps using only `jj` commands where possible.

todos.content: Initialize jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Set repository-level jj config for signing and user identity
Create audit.log and commit it with jj
Verify commit appears in jj log

output.output:
Initialized repo in "."
Hint: Running `git clean -xdf` will remove `.jj/`!

todos.content: Initialize jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Set repository-level jj config for signing and user identity
Create audit.log and commit it with jj
Verify commit appears in jj log

input.command: jj config set --repo signing.behavior own && jj config set --repo signing.backend gpg && jj config set --repo user.name "Security Admin" && jj config set --repo user.email "admin@security.local" && jj config list --repo

output.output:
signing.behavior = "own"
signing.backend = "gpg"
user.name = "Security Admin"
user.email = "admin@security.local"
Warning: This setting will only impact future commits.
The author of the working copy will stay " <>".
To change the working copy author, use "jj metaedit --update-author"
Warning: This setting will only impact future commits.
The author of the working copy will stay " <>".
To change the working copy author, use "jj metaedit --update-author"

todos.content: Initialize jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Set repository-level jj config for signing and user identity
Create audit.log and commit it with jj
Verify commit appears in jj log

input.command: touch audit.log && jj metaedit --update-author && jj commit -m "Add audit log"
find /home/user/secure_repo/.jj -maxdepth 3 -type f | sort | xargs -r -I{} sh -c 'printf "--- %s\n" "$1"; cat "$1" 2>/dev/null || true' sh {}

output.output:

todos.content: Initialize jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Set repository-level jj config for signing and user identity
Create audit.log and commit it with jj
Verify commit appears in jj log

input.command:
mkdir -p /root/.config/jj/repos/d24e7b1dd53e8167bcc7 && cat > /root/.config/jj/repos/d24e7b1dd53e8167bcc7/config.toml <<'EOF'
signing.behavior = "own"
signing.backend = "gpg"
user.name = "Security Admin"
user.email = "admin@security.local"
EOF
jj config list --repo

todos.content: Initialize jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Set repository-level jj config for signing and user identity
Create audit.log and commit it with jj
Verify commit appears in jj log

input.command: touch audit.log && jj commit -m "Add audit log"
GIT_TRACE=1 JJ_TRACE=1 jj commit -m "Add audit log" 2>&1 || true

output.output:
Internal error: Unexpected error from backend
Caused by:
1: Could not write object of type commit
2: Signing error
3: Failed to run GPG
4: No such file or directory (os error 2)

todos.content: Initialize jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Set repository-level jj config for signing and user identity
Create audit.log and commit it with jj
Verify commit appears in jj log

input.result:
Completed as far as the environment allows:

- Initialized a new jj repo at `/home/user/secure_repo` using `jj git init`.
- Applied repository-scoped jj configuration with:
  - `signing.behavior = "own"`
  - `signing.backend = "gpg"`
  - `user.name = "Security Admin"`
  - `user.email = "admin@security.local"`
- Created the empty file `audit.log`.

Verification findings:
- `jj config path --repo` on this jj version resolves repo config to `/root/.config/jj/repos/d24e7b1dd53e8167bcc7/config.toml`, and `jj config list --repo` confirms the settings are active.
- The commit could not be created because the required signing backend is unavailable in this environment. `jj commit -m "Add audit log"` fails with:
  - `Signing error`
  - `Failed to run GPG`
  - `No such file or directory (os error 2)`
- `which gpg` returns no result, so `gpg` is not installed and `jj log` cannot show the new commit because it was never created.

Commands used:
- `jj git init`
- `jj config set --repo signing.behavior own`
- `jj config set --repo signing.backend gpg`
- `jj config set --repo user.name "Security Admin"`
- `jj config set --repo user.email "admin@security.local"`
- `jj config path --repo`
- `jj config list --repo`
- `jj commit -m "Add audit log"`

Current state:
- Repo exists: `/home/user/secure_repo`
- File exists: `/home/user/secure_repo/audit.log`
- Secure signing policy is enforced at repo scope
- No commit was created due to missing `gpg`

If you want help or want to report this environment issue:
- Help: https://getpochi.com/discord
- Bug reports: https://github.com/TabbyML/pochi/issues
